info.title: AssetPay Public API
info.description: Public trading API - skin deposits, withdrawals, pricing, client endpoints, and identity verification.

tags.name: Client Authentication
tags.description: Client token issuance for trading operations

tags.name: Trading
tags.description: Core trading operations (trade URL check, pricing)

tags.name: Verification
tags.description: Client identity verification via Signicat PictureID (document + selfie)

BearerAuth.description: Merchant or admin JWT access token

ClientToken.description: Client JWT token issued via authenticate-client

post.description: Creates a client JWT token for a Steam user. Requires a merchant API key with CORE_ACCESS scope. The returned token is used by the client for inventory, deposit, and withdrawal endpoints.

400.description: Bad Request - validation failed or invalid input

429.description: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded

post.description: Validates a Steam trade URL and checks the user's trade eligibility (bans, escrow, privacy settings). Requires API key with CORE_ACCESS scope. Results are cached for 1 hour unless forceRefresh is true.

400.description: Bad Request - validation failed or invalid input

429.description: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded

get.description: Returns the current price book for a given game. Each item carries a `deposit` price (what AssetPay pays for the item) and a `withdraw` price (fee-inclusive buy price you can pass straight back to /secure/buy). For CS2, `withdraw` is split into `standard` and `instant` delivery and each entry includes the Skinshark catalog `itemId`; for Rust, `withdraw` is a single value. Doppler items are returned as a single entry with a `phases[]` breakdown (per-phase deposit + standard withdraw); the top-level `deposit`/`withdraw` carry the floor as a default, and `withdraw.instant` is the any-phase instant price (instant delivery is not phase-specific). Withdraw prices already include your merchant fee. Requires API key with CORE_ACCESS scope.

itemId.description: Skinshark catalog item id, null when no withdraw floor

accepted.description: Whether the item is accepted for deposit (deposit price > 0)

deposit.description: Deposit offer price in USD (what we pay for the item)

withdraw.description: Fee-inclusive buy price per delivery mode; null when unavailable

accepted.description: Whether this phase is accepted for deposit (deposit price > 0)

deposit.description: Deposit offer price in USD for this phase (what we pay)

withdraw.description: Fee-inclusive standard buy price in USD for this phase; null when unavailable. Instant delivery is not phase-specific - see the item's withdraw.instant.

phases.description: Present for Doppler items: per-phase deposit + standard withdraw prices. The top-level deposit/withdraw carry the floor (cheapest phase / grouped floor) as a default.

accepted.description: Whether the item is accepted for deposit (deposit price > 0)

deposit.description: Deposit offer price in USD (what we pay for the item)

withdraw.description: Fee-inclusive buy price in USD; null when unavailable

400.description: Bad Request - validation failed or invalid input

429.description: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded

get.description: Fetches a Steam account's inventory. The merchant passes a tradeUrl per request and AssetPay derives the steamId from it - no client authentication step is needed. Use this to discover items the merchant can sell to AssetPay via /secure/sell.

assetId.description: Raw Steam assetid. Always present on inventory listings and deposit trade items. Omitted on withdraw items until the item lands in the recipient's Steam inventory.

name.description: Omitted on bare withdraw items where AssetPay has no Steam-side metadata yet

error.description: Failure code for a failed withdraw item (withdrawals only)

reference.description: Marketplace product reference. On a market listing this is the parent itemId you queried `/market/item` with (hex, e.g. "0010f8d48278ccb8"); the listing-specific id is the `id` field above.

previewToken.description: Steam inspect/preview token for rendering the item's in-game preview. Present on inventory and autoseller-pool (self-owned) items; absent on third-party Skinshark listings.

botInfo.description: Holding bot identity. Present on autoseller-pool marketplace items.

400.description: Bad Request - validation failed or invalid input

424.description: Failed Dependency - Steam could not serve the request (inventory fetch failed, temporarily unavailable, or rate-limited upstream). The caller is not at fault; retry shortly.

429.description: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded

post.description: Initiates a deposit-style trade where the merchant sends their own Steam items to AssetPay in exchange for USD wallet credit. Pricing must match the latest /secure/prices snapshot. Webhooks fire on state changes.

offerID.description: Steam trade offer id. Present once the offer is sent.

status.description: Trade status. In-flight: initiated, pending, active, hold. Terminal: completed, failed, canceled, declined, reverted. Deposit flow: initiated → active → hold → completed. Withdraw flow: initiated → pending → active → hold → completed.

assetId.description: Raw Steam assetid. Always present on inventory listings and deposit trade items. Omitted on withdraw items until the item lands in the recipient's Steam inventory.

name.description: Omitted on bare withdraw items where AssetPay has no Steam-side metadata yet

error.description: Failure code for a failed withdraw item (withdrawals only)

reference.description: Marketplace product reference. On a market listing this is the parent itemId you queried `/market/item` with (hex, e.g. "0010f8d48278ccb8"); the listing-specific id is the `id` field above.

previewToken.description: Steam inspect/preview token for rendering the item's in-game preview. Present on inventory and autoseller-pool (self-owned) items; absent on third-party Skinshark listings.

botInfo.description: Holding bot identity. Present on autoseller-pool marketplace items.

pendingCredit.description: Amount held for hold period in USD

error.description: Trade-level failure reason. Withdraw: a TradeFailureCode, set only when the whole trade failed with one shared cause. Deposit: a Steam job error key.

400.description: Bad Request - validation failed or invalid input

424.description: Failed Dependency - Steam could not serve the request (inventory fetch failed, temporarily unavailable, or rate-limited upstream). The caller is not at fault; retry shortly.

429.description: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded

post.summary: Buy skins from the marketplace (merchant self-trade)
post.description: Initiates a withdraw-style trade where AssetPay buys the requested listings from the upstream marketplace (Skinshark for CS2, RustSkins for Rust) and delivers them to the supplied tradeUrl. The merchant wallet is debited.

offerID.description: Steam trade offer id. Present once the offer is sent.

status.description: Trade status. In-flight: initiated, pending, active, hold. Terminal: completed, failed, canceled, declined, reverted. Deposit flow: initiated → active → hold → completed. Withdraw flow: initiated → pending → active → hold → completed.

error.description: Trade-level failure reason. Withdraw: a TradeFailureCode, set only when the whole trade failed with one shared cause. Deposit: a Steam job error key.

post.summary: Quick-buy skins from the marketplace (merchant self-trade)
post.description: Buys the N cheapest listings of a single item at or below a per-unit ceiling. CS2 only. `itemId` and the gross per-unit `maxPrice` (fee-inclusive) come from /secure/prices; `amount` is 1-200 and `delivery` is standard or instant. Optionally pin a Doppler `phase` (e.g. 'Ruby') to buy only that phase, priced against the phase floor (not combinable with instant delivery). The trade starts empty and locks the worst-case total; the filled listings (possibly fewer than requested) and the refund of any unfilled / below-ceiling remainder settle asynchronously via the trade webhook.

phase.description: Doppler phase to buy (e.g. 'Ruby'). Priced against the phase floor. Cannot be combined with delivery 'instant'.

200.description: Quick-buy trade initiated (items settle via webhook)

offerID.description: Steam trade offer id. Present once the offer is sent.

status.description: Trade status. In-flight: initiated, pending, active, hold. Terminal: completed, failed, canceled, declined, reverted. Deposit flow: initiated → active → hold → completed. Withdraw flow: initiated → pending → active → hold → completed.

assetId.description: Raw Steam assetid. Always present on inventory listings and deposit trade items. Omitted on withdraw items until the item lands in the recipient's Steam inventory.

name.description: Omitted on bare withdraw items where AssetPay has no Steam-side metadata yet

error.description: Failure code for a failed withdraw item (withdrawals only)

reference.description: Marketplace product reference. On a market listing this is the parent itemId you queried `/market/item` with (hex, e.g. "0010f8d48278ccb8"); the listing-specific id is the `id` field above.

previewToken.description: Steam inspect/preview token for rendering the item's in-game preview. Present on inventory and autoseller-pool (self-owned) items; absent on third-party Skinshark listings.

botInfo.description: Holding bot identity. Present on autoseller-pool marketplace items.

pendingCredit.description: Amount held for hold period in USD

error.description: Trade-level failure reason. Withdraw: a TradeFailureCode, set only when the whole trade failed with one shared cause. Deposit: a Steam job error key.

400.description: Bad Request - validation failed or invalid input

429.description: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded

post.summary: Cancel an undelivered buy item (merchant self-trade)
post.description: Requests cancellation of a single undelivered item from a merchant self-trade buy. Skinshark (CS2) items only - Rust cannot be cancelled. The item must be at least 30 minutes old and not yet in a terminal state. On success the refund flows back via the trade webhook; the response only confirms the cancellation was accepted.

schema.description: Internal trade id or the externalId supplied at creation.

parameters.description: Internal trade id or the externalId supplied at creation.
parameters.name: tradeId

post.description: Initiates a deposit trade where the client sends skins to the platform. Each item must include the itemId from inventory and the agreed price (USD). Supports instant crediting (isInstant=true) where eligible, or full hold (isInstant=false). Uses distributed locks to prevent race conditions per Steam user.

post.description:
Initiates a Signicat PictureID verification (document scan + selfie). Returns a `captureUrl` the client should open in a browser or webview to complete the capture. An optional `email` can be provided which is stored on the client record for future identity linking.

**Limits:**
- Returns `VERIFICATION_ALREADY_VERIFIED` if the account is already verified (level >= 1).
- Returns `VERIFICATION_IN_PROGRESS` if an active process was created within the last 30 minutes.
- Returns `VERIFICATION_NOT_AVAILABLE` if Signicat credentials are not configured.

200.description: Verification started  open `captureUrl` in the client.

captureUrl.description: URL to redirect/open for document + selfie capture

400.description: Bad Request - validation failed or invalid input

429.description: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded

get.description: Returns the current verification level of the authenticated client, their associated email, the most recent Signicat process (if any), and the linked verified identity (if any).

email.description: Email address associated with this account

captureUrl.description: URL the client should open to complete document capture

latestProcess.description: Most recent verification attempt, or null if none

documentCountry.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the document

linkedIdentity.description: Verified identity linked to this account, or null if not yet verified

429.description: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded

post.description:
Links the authenticated client's account to an existing verified identity via email match. Use this after a successful verification completes for another account with the same email.

No request body is needed  the identity is looked up automatically by the client's email address (set during the `/start` call).

**Requirements:**
- The client must have an email set on their account (provided during `/start`).
- A verified identity with a matching email must exist (case-insensitive).
- The identity must not be banned.
- The identity must not have reached its maximum linked account limit.

On success, the account's `verificationLevel` is immediately upgraded to `1` (picture_id).

200.description: Account successfully linked to the verified identity

400.description: Bad Request - validation failed or invalid input

429.description: Too Many Requests - rate limit exceeded
